mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels indicates an extremely accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings and encourages risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS value 0.14 is near zero lower bound
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate, such as 7.6%, signals a depressed labor market. If this rate begins to consistently decline from such elevated levels, it indicates a labor market recovery, which typically precedes and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7.0% and a 3-month moving average decline of at least 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE meets the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales, combined with an extremely low federal funds rate, indicate strong consumer demand supported by highly accommodative monetary conditions. This dual tailwind boosts corporate revenues and investor sentiment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where both RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts, particularly after a period of weakness, serves as a leading indicator of renewed economic activity and consumer confidence. This pickup in construction translates into broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average shows an increase of at least 5% from its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods following the HOUST trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as reflected by non-farm payrolls, signifies a healthy and expanding labor market. This sustained employment growth underpins consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production consistently above its 2017 base level and showing upward momentum indicates robust manufacturing activity and broader economic expansion. This strength in the supply side of the economy typically correlates with positive S&P 500 performance.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where INDPRO meets the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low inflation (CPIAUCSL) and high unemployment (UNRATE) provides the Federal Reserve ample justification for maintaining or implementing accommodative monetary policy. This environment supports asset prices by lowering real rates and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as the economy recovers without immediate inflation concerns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both low inflation and high unemployment conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a robust and expanding economy, which directly translates into increasing corporate revenues and profits. This fundamental economic strength drives the S&P 500 higher during periods of clear economic expansion.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of periods when GDPC1 meets the sustained growth trigger.
